g04_b73.note:
It's declarational, legal-like section -- it uses lines to emphasize meaning. 
"Каждый человек имеет право жить не в страхе, не в рабстве, не во лжи, а в Свете — как в естественной среде своей души." is not the same as "а в Свете" on a new line -- clear contrast, opposition. But regarding line-by-line conflict -- "Это право включает:" can be considered questionable (it works fine both as prose and lineated, the only reason I give it lineated is that it's style-coherent with surrounding paragraphs/sections, and it's a real factor when there's bit repetition of structure like here with "Articles" or with questions -- as it can be thought that generally, author tries to style "collective-style" content similarly, and thus the collective inference is a bit simpler, given we can prefer consistency when not sure. In-context judgment -- is book or a section prose heavy? is book line-heavy?
But regarding "Жизнь в Свете — не привилегия избранных, ... Кто он" stanza -- it's opposition/contrast, just like the first paragraph.

Without period, it's clear that it can't be the same "line". And it's not about lineated/prose? So, if my suspicion is right -- then both work here. Judge in-context.
